返回本批次

MEMS 阵列芯片可以投射沙粒大小的视频

MEMS Array Chip Can Project Video the Size of a Grain of Sand

spectrum.ieee.org 作者 bookofjoe 分数 82 评论 36 2026/04/13 22:26
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 据许多估计，量子计算机将需要数百万个量子比特才能实现其在网络安全、药物开发和其他行业的潜在应用。问题是，任何想要同时控制数百万个某种量子位的人都会遇到试图控制数百万个激光束的问题。这正是从事 MITRE 量子登月项目的科学家所面临的挑战，该项目汇集了来自 MITRE、麻省理工学院、科罗拉多大学博尔德分校和桑迪亚国家实验室的科学家。他们开发的解决方案以图像投影技术的形式出现，他们意识到该技术也可以解决增强现实、生物医学成像和其他领域的许多其他挑战。该设备是一个一平方毫米的光子芯片，能够将蒙娜丽莎投影到小于两个人类卵细胞大小的区域。 “当我们开始时，我们肯定不会想到我们会开发出一种可能彻底改变成像的技术，”量子登月项目的领导者之一、科罗拉多大学博尔德分校的量子工程学教授马特·艾兴菲尔德（Matt Eichenfield）说，该项目是一个专注于开发可扩展的基于钻石的量子计算机的合作研究项目。他们的芯片每秒能够投射 6860 万个单独的光点，称为可扫描像素，以区别于物理像素。这是微机电系统 (MEMS) 微镜阵列等先前技术能力的五十倍以上。麻省理工学院客座研究员兼 QuEra 计算公司光子学工程师 Henry Wen 表示：“我们现在已经制造出了一个可扫描像素，它达到了衍射允许的绝对极限。”该芯片的显着特征是一系列微小的微尺度悬臂梁，它们响应电压而弯曲远离芯片平面，并充当光的微型“跳台”。光通过波导沿每个悬臂的长度引导，并从其尖端射出。悬臂包含一层薄薄的氮化铝，这是一种在电压下膨胀或收缩的压电材料，从而上下移动微型机器，并使阵列能够在二维区域上扫描光束。尽管团队取得了巨大的成就，但艾兴菲尔德表示，悬臂的设计过程“相当顺利”。每个悬臂由几个亚微米材料层的堆叠组成，并在静止时从平面卷曲约 90 度。为了实现如此高的曲率，该团队利用了制造过程中材料中的物理应力引起的各层收缩和膨胀的差异。首先将材料平坦地沉积到芯片上。然后，去除芯片中悬臂下方的一层，使材料应力发挥作用，将悬臂从芯片上释放并使其卷曲。每个悬臂的顶层还具有一系列垂直于波导延伸的二氧化硅棒，这可以防止悬臂沿其宽度卷曲，同时也改善其长度方向的曲率。微悬臂梁不断摆动，将光线投射到正确的位置。Matt Saha、Y. Henry Wen 等人。比设计芯片本身更具挑战性的是弄清楚实际制作芯片投射图像和视频的细节。参与该项目的 MITRE 研究员安迪·格林斯彭 (Andy Greenspon) 表示，制定同步和计时悬臂运动和光束的过程，以便在正确的时间生成正确的颜色是一项巨大的工作。现在，该团队已经成功地从一个悬臂投影了各种视频，包括电影《查理布朗圣诞节》的片段。该芯片投射了大约 125 微米的蒙娜丽莎图像。Matt Saha、Y. Henry Wen 等人。由于该芯片在任何给定时间间隔内可以投射比任何以前的光束扫描仪更多的点，因此它还可以用于控制量子计算机中更多的量子位。量子登月计划的任务是建造一台可以扩展到数百万量子位的量子计算机。 Wen 解释说，很明显，它需要一种可扩展的方式来控制每一个。该团队意识到，并非每个量子位都需要在每个给定时刻进行控制，而不是每个量子位使用一个激光器。该芯片能够在二维区域上移动光束，这将使它们能够用更少的激光器控制所有量子位。 Wen 认为该芯片可以改进的另一个过程是扫描物体进行 3D 打印。如今，这通常涉及使用单个激光扫描物体的整个表面。然而，新芯片可能会使用数千束激光束。 “我认为现在你可以把原本需要几个小时的过程缩短到几分钟，”文说。温亦是很高兴探索不同悬臂形状的潜力。通过改变垂直于波导的杆的方向，该团队已经能够使悬臂卷曲成螺旋。温说，这种不寻常的形状可能有助于制作细胞生物学或药物开发的芯片实验室。 “很多东西都是成像，用激光扫描某物，要么对其成像，要么刺激某种反应。因此，我们可以让这些滑雪跳台之一不仅向上卷曲，而且实际上向后卷曲，然后四处移动并扫描样本，”温解释道。 “如果你能想象出一种对你有用的结构，我们就应该尝试一下。”

Original Summary

  1. By many estimates, quantum computers will need millions of qubits to realize their potential applications in cybersecurity, drug development, and other industries. The problem is, anyone who has wanted to simultaneously control millions of a certain kind of qubits has run into the problem of trying to control millions of laser beams. That’s exactly the challenge that was faced by scientists working on the MITRE Quantum Moonshot project, which brought together scientists from MITRE, MIT, the University of Colorado at Boulder, and Sandia National Laboratories. The solution they developed came in the form of an image projection technology that they realized could also be the fix for a host of other challenges in augmented reality, biomedical imaging, and elsewhere. The device is a one-square-millimeter photonic chip capable of projecting the Mona Lisa onto an area smaller than the size of two human egg cells. “When we started, we certainly never would have anticipated that we would be making a technology that might revolutionize imaging,” says Matt Eichenfield, one of the leaders of the Quantum Moonshot project, a collaborative research effort focused on developing a scalable diamond-based quantum computer, and a professor of quantum engineering at the University of Colorado at Boulder. Each second, their chip is capable of projecting 68.6 million individual spots of light—called scannable pixels to differentiate them from physical pixels. That’s more than fifty times the capability of previous technology, such as micro-electromechanical systems (MEMS) micromirror arrays. “We have now made a scannable pixel that is at the absolute limit of what diffraction allows,” says Henry Wen, a visiting researcher at MIT and a photonics engineer at QuEra Computing.The chip’s distinguishing feature is an array of tiny micro-scale cantilevers, which curve away from the plane of the chip in response to voltage and act as miniature “ski-jumps” for light. Light is channeled along the length of each cantilever via a waveguide, and exits at its tip. The cantilevers contain a thin layer of aluminum nitride, a piezoelectric which expands or contracts under voltage, thus moving the micromachine up and down and enabling the array to scan beams of light over a two-dimensional area.Despite the magnitude of the team’s achievement, Eichenfield says that the process of engineering the cantilevers was “pretty smooth.” Each cantilever is composed of a stack of several submicrometer layers of material and curls approximately 90 degrees out of the plane at rest. To achieve such a high curvature, the team took advantage of differences in the contraction and expansion of individual layers caused by physical stresses in the material resulting from the fabrication process. The materials are first deposited flat onto the chip. Then, a layer in the chip below the cantilever is removed, allowing the material stresses to take effect, releasing the cantilever from the chip and allowing it to curl out. The top layer of each cantilever also features a series of silicon dioxide bars running perpendicular to the waveguide, which keep the cantilever from curling along its width while also improving its length-wise curvature. A micro-cantilever wiggles and waggles to project light in the right place.Matt Saha, Y. Henry Wen, et al.What was more of a challenge than engineering the chip itself was figuring out the details of actually making the chip project images and videos. Working out the process of synchronizing and timing the cantilevers’ motion and light beams to generate the right colors at the right time was a substantial effort, according to Andy Greenspon, a researcher at MITRE who also worked on the project. Now, the team has successfully projected a variety of videos from a single cantilever, including clips from the movie A Charlie Brown Christmas. The chip projected a roughly 125-micrometer image of the Mona Lisa.Matt Saha, Y. Henry Wen, et al.Because the chip can project so many more spots in any given time interval than any previous beam scanners, it could also be used to control many more qubits in quantum computers. The Quantum Moonshot program’s mission is to build a quantum computer that can be scaled to millions of qubits. So clearly, it needs a scalable way of controlling each one, explains Wen. Instead of using one laser per qubit, the team realized that not every qubit needed to be controlled at every given moment. The chip’s ability to move light beams over a two-dimensional area, would allow them to control all of the qubits with many fewer lasers. Another process that Wen thinks the chip could improve is scanning objects for 3D printing. Today, that typically involves using a single laser to scan over the entire surface of an object. The new chip, however, could potentially employ thousands of laser beams. “I think now you can take a process that would have taken hours and maybe bring it down to minutes,” says Wen. Wen is also excited to explore the potential of different cantilever shapes. By changing the orientations of the bars perpendicular to the waveguide, the team has been able to make the cantilevers curl into helixes. Wen says that such unusual shapes could be useful in making a lab-on-a-chip for cell biology or drug development. “A lot of this stuff is imaging, scanning a laser across something, either to image it or to stimulate some response. And so we could have one of these ski jumps curl not just up, but actually curl back around, and then move around and scan over a sample,” Wen explains. “If you can imagine a structure that will be useful for you, we should try it.”

评论（默认中文）

antimatter15 2026/04/14 02:16
这让我想起 Magic Leap 拥有的原始专利，其中涉及通过一根光纤泵浦光，该光纤由压电元件摆动成螺旋状以投射光（https://kguttag.com/2018/01/06/magic-leap-fiber-scanning-dis...）。
This reminds me of the original patents that Magic Leap had, which involved pumping light through a single optical fiber that was wiggled by piezoelectrics into a spiral to project light (https://kguttag.com/2018/01/06/magic-leap-fiber-scanning-dis...).
在 HN 中查看
nomel 2026/04/14 03:55
看起来就是这样，但有一个“波导”而不是一个摆动的“光纤”。不过，这似乎是对“投影”一词的偷偷使用，因为图像被“投影”的“表面”正是翻转的波导头所追踪的内容，没有投影延伸到波导的末端之外。
Seems what it is, but with a "waveguide" instead of an "optical fiber" wiggling about. Seems like a sneaky use of the word "projection" though, since the "surface" the image is "projected" is just what the flopping waveguide head traces, with no projection extending beyond the end of the waveguide.
在 HN 中查看
reactordev 2026/04/14 09:04
是的，但有人可能会说激光投影仪或液晶投影仪的帧刷新/重绘周期在低速时是相同的。它不仅仅是一个巨大的光球。它要经历一个过程，框架本身必须重新绘制。
Yes but one could argue the frame refresh/redraw cycle of a laser projector or lcd projector is the same at slow speed. It’s not just one giant ball of light. It goes through a process and the frame itself has to redraw.
在 HN 中查看
jmward01 2026/04/14 02:13
我想知道这是否对定制家用芯片/原型设计有影响。我确信一个大问题是振动，但这样的事情至少可以消除对面罩的需要。 （同样，这不是我的领域，所以我确信我正在破坏术语）。它可能会开放家庭晶圆厂的能力。
I wonder if this has implications for custom home chips/prototyping. I'm sure a big issue is vibrations but something like this could remove the need for masks at least. (again, not my area so I am clobbering terminology I am sure). It may open up home fab capabilities.
在 HN 中查看
Joel_Mckay 2026/04/14 02:22
一般来说，业余爱好光刻项目已经使用 DMD/DLP 投影仪和一些廉价的光学器件。

惠更斯光学：

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_w0Z2Y5vaAQ

萨姆·泽洛夫：

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Nxz_ENnmgtI

一般来说，制造硅片通常比尝试引导一条晶圆厂生产线便宜得多。 =3

In general, hobby photo-lithography projects already use DMD/DLP projectors, and some inexpensive optics.

Huygens Optics:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_w0Z2Y5vaAQ

Sam Zeloof:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Nxz_ENnmgtI

In general, getting vanity silicon made is usually much less expensive than trying to bootstrap a fab line. =3

在 HN 中查看
jmward01 2026/04/14 04:55
我认为，对于做这样的事情的人来说，一半的乐趣在于弄清楚如何对一家价值数十亿美元的公司进行创新，这样他们就可以以 1/10000 的价格制造出 1/4 的产品。我敢打赌，那里有一些真正具有创新精神的人会找到该工艺中许多昂贵部分的替代品，并找出如何能够在国内生产 2000 年代级别的芯片。但我不是他们中的一员:)
I think half the fun for people that do things like this is figuring out how to out innovate a multi-billion dollar company so that they can make something 1/4th as good but at 1/10000th the price. I bet there are some -really- innovative people out there that would figure alternatives to a lot of the expensive parts of the process and figure out how to be able to produce 2000's level chips at home. I'm not one of them though :)
在 HN 中查看
15155 2026/04/14 07:20
问题是，它们的价格还不到 1/10000 的 1/4。图案化只是整个过程的一部分，而且还不是最困难的部分。
The problem is that they aren't (yet) 1/4th as good for 1/10000th of the price. Patterning is just one part of the process - and not nearly the most difficult one.
在 HN 中查看
volemo 2026/04/14 02:58
我认为滥用注销电子显微镜来回避对掩模的需求也是一个有趣的想法，但是，我相信获得足够质量的晶圆并沉积要蚀刻的层可能是这里更大的挑战。
I think abusing a write-off electron microscope to side step the need for masks is also an interesting idea, however, I believe acquiring wafers of sufficient quality and depositing layers to be etched could be the bigger challenge here.

jacquesm 2026/04/14 03:29

And the clean environment as a whole. That's a massive investment and there are a million ways to mess that up.

bigbadfeline 2026/04/14 05:01

对于家庭晶圆厂来说肯定很难，但社区晶圆厂又如何呢？不一定是地理社区。

> however, I believe acquiring wafers of sufficient quality and depositing layers to be etched could be the bigger challenge here

Definitely hard for a home fab but how about a community fab? Not necessarily a geographic community.

m3kw9 2026/04/14 00:31

jcims 2026/04/14 04:19
看起来你可以把其中一些放在隐形眼镜上，并至少得到一个小型的私人平视显示器。 似乎使用其中的一些（或足够快的扫描速度），您可以有效地构建一个光场以赋予其深度）
Seems like you could put a few of these on a contact lens and minimally get a small private HUD. Seems like with a few of them (or fast enough scanning speed) you could build effectively a light field to give it depth)

CoolThings 2026/04/14 01:48
这可能与增强现实头盔相关。
This might be relevant for Augmented Reality headgear.
在 HN 中查看
foruhar 2026/04/14 08:14

Happy days at the ant colony.
在 HN 中查看
cubefox 2026/04/14 02:06
> 该芯片投射出约 125 微米的蒙娜丽莎图像。

这可能看起来很小（肉眼几乎看不见一个点），但这也是普朗克长度和可观测宇宙直径的几何平均值。实际上是平均尺寸。

> The chip projected a roughly 125-micrometer image of the Mona Lisa.

This may seem small (barely visible as a dot to the naked eye), but that's also the geometric mean of the Planck length and the diameter of the observable universe. So average size actually.

在 HN 中查看
jacquesm 2026/04/14 03:33
我 真的 无法关注你的评论，我一直在努力。您介意对您在这里得到的内容进行更长的解释吗？
I really can't follow your comment and I've been trying. Would you mind a longer explanation of what you're getting at here?
在 HN 中查看
venv 2026/04/14 04:07
它们的意思是 125um = sqrt(a*b)，其中 a 是普朗克长度*，b 是可观测宇宙的大小（我没有验证）。这意味着，125um 是某种中间立场。 *常说是有物理意义的最小长度。
They mean 125um = sqrt(a*b), where a is the Planck length* and b the size of the observable universe (I didn't verify). Implying, 125um is some sort of middle ground. *Often said to be the smallest length with physical meaning.
在 HN 中查看
jacquesm 2026/04/14 05:00
好吧，所以这是一个废话评论。谢谢。你可以对所有不是“普朗克长度”的东西都这么说，它和道格拉斯·亚当斯的“宇宙是空的”一样有用（只是他有幽默感）。

哦，更糟糕的是他们在不同的线程中重复它。

https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47749957

Ok, so it's a bullshit comment. Thank you. You could say this about everything that is not 'Planck length', it's about as useful as Douglas Adams' 'the universe is empty' (only he had a sense of humor).

Oh, even worse they are repeating it in different threads.

https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47749957

jacquesm 2026/04/14 06:09
因为其中一些是明确的选择，而另一些则不是。

你可以批评以色列，我对此没有意见——而且我自己也批评以色列。但说“大多数犹太人都是疯子”绝对是过分的。

你 可以 更笼统地说，关于所有有宗教信仰的人，但你没有这样做，仅供参考，犹太人通常无法选择自己的犹太身份，就像你无法选择自己出生在哪个家庭一样。

你甚至可以说，大多数宗教人士在接受该宗教时别无选择，但大多数人理论上可以选择放弃他们的宗教，如果他们愿意的话， 但你不能停止成为犹太人。这个小细节被融入了宗教，对于那些想要摆脱它的犹太人来说是一个严重的问题——这些人确实存在——但他们无法将自己的身份改变到他们自己认为足够的程度，此外，他们的环境通常也不承认这一点。

为了加深您对此的了解：

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Off_the_derech

和

https://brill.com/display/book/edcoll/9789004331471/BP000006...

我知道这一切都很复杂，也许你真的无法理解这一点，在这种情况下我很抱歉，但在你为谁工作（例如，Palantir、Facebook、OpenAI 或 Twitter）与你出生的家庭之间存在着重大选择。

至于政治信仰：是的，我批评那些为特朗普、普京、内塔尼亚胡及其亲信送水的人，他们的目的是摧毁我们所知道的世界，如果你帮助实现这一目标，我相信你就是问题的一部分。

Because some of these are clear choices, others are not.

You can be critical of Israel and I'm fine with that - and I'm plenty critical of Israel myself. But to say 'Most Jews are lunatics' is absolutely beyond the pale.

You could say this in more general terms about all people that are religious but you didn't do that and FYI Jews don't generally have a choice about their Jewishness, just like you don't have any choice about which family you were born into.

You could even make the case that most religious people had no choice in their adoption of that religion, but most people have the theoretical option of letting go of their religion if they so desire, but you can not stop being a Jew. This little detail was baked into the religion and it is a serious problem for those that are Jewish and that wish to get away from it - and these people really do exist -, but they can not change their identity to a degree that they themselves would recognize as sufficient, besides, their environment usually also does not recognize it.

In the interest of furthering your knowledge about this:

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Off_the_derech

and

https://brill.com/display/book/edcoll/9789004331471/BP000006...

I get all this is complicated, and maybe you really can't follow this in which case my apologies but there is a significant choice between who you work for (say, Palantir, Facebook, OpenAI or Twitter) vs what family you are born into.

As for political beliefs: yes, I'm critical of those that carry water for Trump, Putin, Netanyahu and their cronies, they're out to destroy the world as we know it and if you help enable that you are imnsho part of the problem.

cordwainersmith 2026/04/14 01:05
你如何将视频投影仪安装到这么小的东西上，物理感觉他们不应该合作。
How do you even fit a video projector onto something that small, the physics feel like they shouldn't cooperate.

kylehotchkiss 2026/04/14 03:57
听起来这会对光纤产生有趣的影响？
Sounds like this will have interesting fiber-optic implications?
在 HN 中查看
cyberax 2026/04/14 02:44
这实际上已经足够接近来操纵光的_相位_了！这样做就可以创建真正的全息图。

或者是替代的真正的增强现实眼镜，不限于一个焦平面。

This is actually getting close enough to manipulate the _phase_ of light! And doing that would allow creating true holograms.

Or alternative true augmented reality glasses that are not limited to one focal plane.

darfo 2026/04/13 22:40
凉爽的。现在我可以向我的果蝇播放视频了！ /秒

Srsly标题应该是“沙粒大小的MEMS阵列芯片可以投影视频”

不是

“MEMS 阵列芯片可以投射沙粒大小的视频”

Cool. Now I can show videos to my fruit flies! /s

Srsly title should be "MEMS Array Chip the Size of a Grain of Sand Can Project Video"

not

"MEMS Array Chip Can Project Video the Size of a Grain of Sand"

在 HN 中查看
projektfu 2026/04/14 01:16
它实际上是大约0.125mm的投影，而不是芯片的尺寸。 但更多的是关于转向激光器，这正是他们真正想做的。
It is actually about a 0.125mm projection, not the size of the chip. But more about steering lasers, which is really what they wanted to do.
